extra.message: User-controlled input from request.GET.get('p') is passed without sanitization to os.path.join() and then to open(). An attacker can supply '../../../etc/passwd' or similar path traversal sequences to read arbitrary files from the filesystem outside the intended base_path directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-supplied input from request.POST.get('first_name') is base64-decoded and passed directly to exec(). This allows an attacker to execute arbitrary Python code on the server by submitting a base64-encoded Python payload, enabling full remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Fallback Python 2 path also passes base64-decoded user input from request.POST.get('first_name') directly to exec(). Same remote code execution vulnerability as line 72.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input from request.GET.get('url') is passed directly to redirect() without any validation or whitelist check. An attacker can supply an arbitrary external URL (e.g., https://evil.com) to redirect victims to a phishing or malware site.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: The unvalidated_forward view resolves the 'fwd' query parameter directly to a Python function name via globals() and calls it without any authentication or authorization check. An attacker can forward execution to any globally accessible function (e.g., 'admin') by supplying its name as the 'fwd' parameter, bypassing access controls entirely.

The missing_access_control view renders the admin template when request.GET.get('action') == 'admin', with no authentication or session check. Any unauthenticated user can access admin functionality by appending '?action=admin' to the URL.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The dor_user_profile view accepts a userid URL parameter and returns or modifies the corresponding user's profile data without verifying that the requesting user is authorized to access or modify that userid. Any user can read or overwrite another user's name and email by changing the userid in the URL.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The csrf_image view is decorated with @csrf_exempt, disabling Django's CSRF protection for this endpoint. Combined with the globally disabled CsrfViewMiddleware in settings.py (commented out), this view is fully vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

User-controlled query parameter 'qs' is rendered inside a JavaScript block using the |safe filter, bypassing Django's auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by breaking out of the string literal (e.g., qs=\";alert(1)//), leading to reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: URL path parameter 'path' is rendered inside a JavaScript block using the |safe filter, bypassing Django's auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript through the URL path segment, leading to reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled query parameter 'qs' is rendered into an HTML attribute value using the |safe filter, bypassing Django's auto-escaping. An attacker can close the attribute and inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g., qs=\"><script>alert(1)</script>), leading to reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: DEBUG = True is set in the Django settings file. In debug mode, Django exposes detailed tracebacks including local variable values, settings, and installed apps on any unhandled exception, leaking sensitive configuration data to any visitor who triggers a 500 error.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Django's CsrfViewMiddleware is commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES. This disables CSRF protection globally for the entire application, making all state-changing endpoints vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

Django's XFrameOptionsMiddleware is commented out. Without this middleware, the application does not set the X-Frame-Options HTTP header, making all pages embeddable in iframes and therefore vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: The Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in the settings file as a static string. This key is used for cryptographic signing of sessions, CSRF tokens, and password reset links. Any developer or attacker with source access can forge these tokens or decrypt sensitive data.
